name: D & F Lending Corporation
description_short: D & F Lending Corporation is a Florida-based personal loan lender established in 2006, operating as an active profit corporation with consistent annual compliance filings.
description_long: D & F Lending Corporation is a Florida profit corporation that was originally filed on February 17, 2006, and became effective on February 14, 2006. The company is registered with the Florida Department of State, Division of Corporations under document number P06000024359 and maintains an active status with Federal EIN 20-4353156. Based in Miami at 7270 NW 12th Street, the company has maintained its principal and mailing address since a change recorded on June 11, 2019. The company experienced a reinstatement event on September 29, 2017, and has since maintained consistent annual report filings through 2026, demonstrating ongoing operational compliance. The company is managed by Francisco J. Delmonte Jr. as President and Diana Martinez as Vice President, both operating from the Miami address. D & F Lending Corporation operates within the personal loans category, offering financial products and services to consumers seeking personal financing solutions. The company maintains documented corporate governance with filed annual reports dating back to its inception, showing a long operational history in the consumer lending space. Their consistent annual report filings—including an amended annual report filed on August 23, 2024—indicate active business operations and regulatory compliance with Florida state requirements. The company's 20-year operational history and current active status suggest established market presence, though specific product details, lending terms, rates, and underwriting criteria are not publicly available through the corporation registration records. As a Florida-registered entity with long-standing compliance history, D & F Lending Corporation represents a registered lending operation; however, prospective borrowers would need to contact the company directly to evaluate loan products, eligibility requirements, interest rates, and other borrowing terms that are not disclosed in public registration documents.
pros: Active Florida profit corporation with ACTIVE status maintained through consistent annual compliance filings
20-year operational history since original filing in February 2006, demonstrating established market presence
Successfully reinstated in September 2017 with continuous annual report filings through 2026, showing regulatory adherence
Established corporate structure with identified officers (Francisco J. Delmonte Jr., President; Diana Martinez, VP) providing transparency
Maintains consistent principal and mailing address in Miami since June 2019, indicating stable business operations
Filed amended annual report in August 2024, showing active current business management and oversight
Long-standing compliance record with Florida Department of State Division of Corporations
cons: No publicly available information about specific loan products, terms, rates, or lending criteria on registration documents
Limited online presence—no operational website content provided beyond corporate registration data
No disclosed information about customer service accessibility, application process, or funding timelines
No available consumer reviews, ratings, or third-party verification of service quality
Vague business description in public records; specific lending focus and loan amounts not detailed
best_for: Borrowers seeking personal loans from an established, state-registered Florida lender with 20+ year history
Consumers in Miami or Florida who prefer local lending institutions with documented compliance records
Applicants who value regulatory oversight and verified corporate standing through state registration
diagnosis: D & F Lending Corporation is a legitimate, state-registered personal lender with strong compliance credentials and 20 years of operational history in Florida. The primary caveat is that detailed information about loan products, rates, terms, and specific lending criteria is not publicly available through corporate records; interested borrowers must contact the company directly to assess whether their products and terms meet individual needs.
services: Corporate compliance and regulatory filing maintenance
Lending products (specific terms and amounts not publicly detailed)
